ALL SPECIALTIES / DIETITIAN / TENNESSEE

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Tennessee Dietitian License

Tennessee Dietitian license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-02-25 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Dietitian applications in Tennessee run through Board of Dietitian/Nutritionist Examiners. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Official College Transcripts - Sent directly from the degree-granting institution, showing degree and date awarded.
  • CDR Registration Card - Copy of your current, active Commission on Dietetic Registration credential.
  • Supervised Practice Verification - Documentation verifying completion of ACEND-accredited supervised practice hours.
  • Passport-Style Photograph - Recent photo meeting standard passport specifications.
  • Criminal Background Check Authorization - Mandatory background screening as part of the application process.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 3 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Tennessee does not offer formal licensure by endorsement or reciprocity. Out-of-state dietitians must apply for a new Tennessee license through the standard application process, which requires current CDR registration, a complete application, and a background check. Holding an active CDR credential satisfies the education and examination requirements.

  1. STEP 1

    Register an Account with the Licensure and Regulatory System

    Navigate to the Licensure and Regulatory System portal at apps.health.tn.gov/Licensure/ and click 'Register a New Account' to create a user ID and password.

    Fee: $140 · Timeline: 3 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Complete and Submit the Online Application

    Log in to the Licensure and Regulatory System, select the Dietitian/Nutritionist application, complete all required sections, and submit it electronically.

    Fee: $140 · Timeline: 3 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Submit Required Documentation

    Upload supporting documents through the Licensure and Regulatory System, including official transcripts verifying a qualifying degree, documentation of a minimum of 900 hours of supervised practice experience, and verification of successful completion of the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) examination.

    Fee: $140 · Timeline: 3 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Pay the Application Fee

    Remit the required initial application fee of $100.00 through the online Licensure and Regulatory System.

    Fee: $140 · Timeline: 3 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Await Board Review and Licensure

    The Tennessee Board of Dietitian/Nutritionist Examiners will review the submitted application and all accompanying documentation. Upon approval, the license will be issued.

    Fee: $140 · Timeline: 3 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$140
Background check
$0 (included in application)
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$140

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board of Dietitian/Nutritionist Examiners

Renewal

Dietitian renewal in Tennessee follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years (by birth month) — no CE required for state renewal
Renewal fee
$90
Continuing education
See board

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does a Tennessee dietitian license cost?

The initial application fee is $140, which includes the state regulatory fee. The CDR exam costs an additional $200. The biennial renewal fee is $90, due by the last day of your birth month.

Does Tennessee require continuing education for dietitians?

Tennessee does not mandate state-level continuing education for license renewal. However, to maintain your CDR registration — which is required for state licensure — you must complete 75 continuing professional education units every five years.

Can I practice in Tennessee while waiting for my license?

Tennessee offers a temporary permit for $5 for applicants who have CDR authorization to test. This permit allows practice while you await your exam results, provided your application is complete and on file with the Board.

Does Tennessee have reciprocity for dietitians?

Tennessee has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, dietitians still apply by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Tennessee?

Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

Can I start working in Tennessee while my endorsement application is processing?

Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$140

Processing time

3 Weeks

Fingerprinting

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Not available

National exam

Registration Examination for Dietitians

Compact

Yes

Renewal

Every 2 years (by birth month) — no CE required for state renewal · $90

Licensing board

Board of Dietitian/Nutritionist Examiners

tn.gov/health/health-program-areas/health-professional-boards/dietitian-board.html
